qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe Java deserialization in HFClient.deSerializeChannel enables remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same root cause as the advisory: ObjectInputStream.readObject() called on untrusted byte arrays without ObjectInputFilter in Channel.readObject()/deSerializeChannel() in fabric-sdk-java. The advisory identifies CVE-2026-41586 / CWE-502 in Channel.java's readObject and deSerializeChannel methods, which is exactly what this finding reports at HFClient.java:334-335 and Channel.java:362.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded memory allocation and integer truncation in chaincode package tar entry processing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (DoS via unbounded memory allocation in LifecycleChaincodePackage tar processing) from the advisory's deserialization RCE (ObjectInputStream.readObject() without filter in Channel.java). Different code location, different root cause, different CWE.

selected_finding.title: Unsafe Java deserialization in HFClient.deSerializeChannel enables remote code execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The unsafe_deserialization_rce finding matches the advisory root cause: ObjectInputStream.readObject() on untrusted bytes without ObjectInputFilter in Channel.java/deSerializeChannel(). PoC execution confirmed the deserialization callback was triggered (readObject() executed arbitrary code, producing proof output EVOHUNT_DESERIALIZATION_RCE_PROOF_1781162385511), but verify.sh failed to confirm the proof file at the expected path, so exploit_success is not fully satisfied. AT:P because exploitation requires a deployment that accepts Channel bytes from an external source (shared cache, database, file upload), which is a setup-dependent condition.
evidence: Advisory identifies Channel.readObject()/deSerializeChannel() calling ObjectInputStream.readObject() without ObjectInputFilter - same as finding's code locations HFClient.java:334-335, Channel.java:362
PoC run log line 730: '[evil-gadget] Executed command via readObject() deserialization callback' - confirms bug activation
PoC run log line 732: 'PROOF: File content = EVOHUNT_DESERIALIZATION_RCE_PROOF_1781162385511' - deserialization callback executed and produced proof output
PoC run log line 736: '[evohunt-verify] FAIL: Proof file was not created' - formal exploit verification failed
blockers_to_higher_tier: verify.sh reported FAIL - the proof file was not found at the expected path after exploit execution, so exploit_success_predicate is not fully satisfied by runtime artifacts
Maven build of fabric-sdk-java failed inside container, so the exploit ran without the actual SDK JAR on classpath

poc_executions.observed_effect: ObjectInputStream.readObject() deserialized a malicious Evil object; the readObject() callback executed arbitrary shell commands and printed proof string to stdout, but verify.sh failed to locate the proof file at expected path
